content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both touch edit()/Editor in the same two files, so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposing a sequential split instead: I'll implement multi-file filename support first (small, localized changes to edit_file->edit_files signature and the edit()/Editor call sites), send DONE when finished, then you layer process_priority on top of my changes rather than reverting them. I will avoid touching process_priority-related code; please avoid reverting the filename Iterable support when you edit. Sound OK?

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished multi-file support: renamed Editor.edit_file -> edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]), joins multiple quoted filenames for the editor subprocess call, edit() now accepts filename: str | Iterable[str] | None with type overloads, docstring/versionchanged 8.2.0 added. I only added the new edit_files method and changed the filename normalization block at the end of termui.edit() (isinstance check + list conversion + ed.edit_files(filenames) call) — signature/init untouched otherwise, so there's room at the end of the parameter lists for your process_priority param. All existing tests pass. Go ahead and layer your changes on top.
